name: PayPal
description: PayPal Payments API errors are returned as `name` + `message` + `details` triples. Common production failures: INSTRUMENT_DECLINED (card declined), AUTHENTICATION_FAILURE (expired OAuth token), VALIDATION_ERROR, RATE_LIMIT_REACHED.
longDescription: PayPal uses OAuth 2.0 client credentials — bearer tokens expire (~9h) and need refresh, which is the single most common PayPal integration bug. Errors come as 4xx/5xx with structured JSON body containing `name`, `message`, `debug_id`, and `details[]`. Sandbox uses separate environment with sandbox buyer/seller accounts. Webhook verification uses asymmetric signatures (PayPal signs, you verify) — more moving parts than Stripe's HMAC.

name: Stripe
description: Stripe processes payments for millions of businesses. Its API surfaces decline reasons, webhook signatures, idempotency conflicts, and rate limits as structured error codes.
longDescription: Stripe error codes split into card errors (`card_declined`, `expired_card`, `insufficient_funds`), API errors (`api_connection_error`, `idempotency_error`), webhook errors (`signature_verification_failed`), and rate limits (HTTP 429). Most production incidents trace to webhook signature drift, idempotency key reuse, or PaymentIntent state mismatches.

name: AWS
description: AWS spans 200+ services, each with its own error catalogue. Common failures: IAM permissions (AccessDeniedException), S3 (NoSuchBucket, 403), Lambda (timeout, throttling), DynamoDB (ProvisionedThroughputExceededException).
longDescription: AWS error codes follow a service-prefixed pattern: `S3.NoSuchBucket`, `IAM.AccessDenied`, `Lambda.TooManyRequestsException`. Most production issues stem from IAM policy gaps, region mismatches, or service quotas. Use CloudTrail to trace the exact API call that failed and the principal that made it.

name: Google Cloud
description: Google Cloud surfaces errors via gRPC status codes (PERMISSION_DENIED, RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, UNAVAILABLE) and HTTP error responses. Common failure modes: IAM, quota limits, regional outages.
longDescription: GCP errors map gRPC codes to HTTP status (PERMISSION_DENIED → 403, RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED → 429, UNAVAILABLE → 503). Most issues trace to project-level IAM bindings, service-specific quotas, or default credentials misconfiguration. `gcloud auth application-default login` solves a surprising number of local-dev errors.

name: GitHub
description: GitHub API errors come in three flavours: REST (HTTP status), GraphQL (typed errors), and Actions (workflow failures). Rate limits (5000/hr authenticated) and SSO authorization are the most common production blockers.
longDescription: GitHub API rate-limit responses include `X-RateLimit-Remaining` and `X-RateLimit-Reset` headers — always honour them with exponential backoff. Secondary rate limits trigger on rapid sequential requests. SAML SSO errors (403) require token re-authorization in org settings. Webhook delivery failures are visible in Settings → Webhooks → Recent deliveries.

name: OpenAI
description: OpenAI API errors: insufficient_quota (402), rate_limit_exceeded (429), invalid_request_error, model_not_found, context_length_exceeded. Most production pain comes from token limits and tier-based RPM caps.
longDescription: OpenAI rate limits are tier-based: free, tier 1, tier 2, etc. Limits apply to RPM (requests/min), TPM (tokens/min), and RPD (requests/day) per model. `context_length_exceeded` triggers when prompt + max_tokens > model's context window — count tokens with tiktoken before sending. `insufficient_quota` is a billing issue, not a rate limit.

name: Anthropic
description: Anthropic Claude API errors: invalid_request_error (400), authentication_error (401), permission_error (403), rate_limit_error (429), api_error (500), overloaded_error (529).
longDescription: Anthropic uses typed error objects: `{type, message}`. Rate limits are tier-based RPM/TPM/ITPM (input tokens per minute). `overloaded_error` (529) is server-side capacity — retry with backoff. Long messages (>200k tokens) require Claude with extended context. Tool use errors require strict JSON Schema in the tool definition.

name: Vercel
description: Vercel deploy errors: build failures, function timeouts (FUNCTION_INVOCATION_TIMEOUT), DNS issues (DEPLOYMENT_NOT_FOUND), edge runtime errors. Most issues trace to env vars, build commands, or function size limits.
longDescription: Vercel function errors carry codes like `FUNCTION_INVOCATION_TIMEOUT`, `FUNCTION_PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE`, `EDGE_FUNCTION_INVOCATION_FAILED`. Hobby plan: 10s function timeout, 4.5MB payload. Pro: 60s, 4.5MB. Use streaming responses for long-running operations. Build errors usually point at missing env vars (set them in dashboard, not just locally).

name: Cloudflare
description: Cloudflare error codes start with 5xx (520-530 series) for origin issues, 1xxx for Workers, 1000-series for security blocks. Most common: 520 (web server returned unknown error), 522 (origin timeout), 524 (origin response timeout).
longDescription: Cloudflare 5xx range distinguishes origin from edge: 520 = origin returned malformed response, 522 = origin TCP timeout, 524 = origin took >100s to respond, 525 = SSL handshake failed. Workers errors use 1101 (script threw), 1102 (CPU limit), 1015 (rate limited). Always check both Cloudflare dashboard and origin server logs.

name: Twilio
description: Twilio errors are 5-digit codes (e.g., 21211 invalid 'To' number, 30003 unreachable destination, 20003 authentication error). SMS delivery failures (3xxxx) are the most common production issue.
longDescription: Twilio error codes group by domain: 1xxxx = REST API, 2xxxx = TwiML/voice, 3xxxx = messaging, 4xxxx = video, 5xxxx = chat. SMS delivery: 30003 (unreachable), 30005 (unknown destination), 30006 (landline), 30007 (carrier filtered). Always check Twilio Console → Logs → Messaging for the exact failure reason on a per-message basis.

name: Shopify
description: Shopify Admin API errors: 401 invalid API key, 402 unpaid plan, 403 scope mismatch, 422 invalid resource, 429 throttled. GraphQL throttling uses cost-based bucket (1000 points, 50/sec leak).
longDescription: Shopify GraphQL throttling differs from REST — every query has a calculated cost, and you have a 1000-point bucket that leaks at 50 points/second. Use `extensions.cost` in responses to monitor. REST 429 includes `Retry-After`. Webhooks deliver up to 19 retries over 48 hours. App proxy auth errors usually mean missing or invalid HMAC.

name: Slack
description: Slack Web API errors: invalid_auth, not_authed, account_inactive, missing_scope, channel_not_found, rate_limited. Tier-based rate limits (Tier 1: 1 req/min, Tier 4: 100+/min).
longDescription: Slack errors return as `{ok: false, error: 'error_code'}` in JSON, even on HTTP 200. Always check `ok` field, not status. Rate limit tiers: Tier 1 (~1/min), Tier 2 (~20/min), Tier 3 (~50/min), Tier 4 (~100/min). Posting messages = Tier 4. Workspace audit = Tier 1. Honour `Retry-After` header.

name: Auth0
description: Auth0 errors: invalid_grant (refresh failed), unauthorized_client, access_denied, login_required, consent_required, mfa_required. Rate limits per Management API endpoint and Authentication API.
longDescription: Auth0 OAuth errors follow RFC 6749. `invalid_grant` after refresh usually means refresh token rotation has invalidated the old token — store the new one. `mfa_required` returns an `mfa_token` to use with `/mfa/challenge`. Rule errors run server-side; check Logs → Failed Login Attempts. Management API rate limits are per-tenant, not per-user.

name: Firebase
description: Firebase errors split by product: Auth (auth/email-already-in-use), Firestore (permission-denied, resource-exhausted), Cloud Functions (deadline-exceeded, unavailable), FCM (registration-token-not-registered).
longDescription: Firebase Auth error codes use namespaced format like `auth/email-already-in-use`. Firestore uses gRPC codes (PERMISSION_DENIED, FAILED_PRECONDITION). Most production failures: missing security rules, FCM token expiration, Cloud Function cold starts hitting deadline, App Check token rejection.

name: Supabase
description: Supabase errors: 401 invalid JWT, 403 RLS policy violation, 406 invalid query, 409 unique constraint, 503 capacity. Most issues are RLS policy mistakes or PostgREST query errors.
longDescription: Supabase wraps Postgres + PostgREST + Auth. RLS policy errors return 403 with hint in response body. JWT issues return 401. PostgREST returns 406 for malformed queries (wrong filters, embed syntax). Always test policies with the SQL editor first. Connection pooler exhaustion shows as 503.

name: Postgres
description: Postgres errors use 5-character SQLSTATE codes: 23505 (unique violation), 23503 (foreign key violation), 40P01 (deadlock), 53300 (too many connections), 57P03 (cannot connect now).
longDescription: Postgres SQLSTATE codes group by class: 08 (connection), 22 (data), 23 (integrity constraint), 40 (transaction rollback), 42 (syntax), 53 (insufficient resources). Most production issues: connection pool exhaustion (53300), deadlocks (40P01), and migration-time constraint violations (23xxx). Use `pg_stat_activity` to debug live.

name: MySQL
description: MySQL errors use numeric codes: 1062 (duplicate entry), 1452 (foreign key fails), 1205 (lock wait timeout), 1213 (deadlock), 2002 (connection refused), 2013 (lost connection during query).
longDescription: MySQL errors fall into client (2xxx) and server (1xxx) ranges. Connection issues (2002, 2003, 2005) are network/credentials. Server errors (1xxx) report SQL or storage engine problems. InnoDB deadlocks (1213) are auto-resolved — retry the failed transaction. Lock wait timeout (1205) usually means a long-running transaction is blocking.

name: Redis
description: Redis errors: WRONGTYPE (operation against wrong type key), MOVED (cluster redirect), ASK (cluster slot migrating), READONLY (replica write attempt), OOM (out of memory), MAXCLIENTS REACHED.
longDescription: Redis Cluster slot errors (MOVED, ASK) are normal — your client should follow them. WRONGTYPE means a key exists with a different type than expected (often a leftover from a previous version). OOM with `maxmemory-policy: noeviction` blocks all writes — pick a sensible eviction policy. CROSSSLOT errors come from multi-key commands across slots in cluster mode.

name: MongoDB
description: MongoDB errors: 11000 (duplicate key), 50 (operation timeout), 89 (network timeout), 13 (auth failure), 121 (document validation), 133 (FailedToSatisfyReadPreference), 134 (ReadConcernMajorityNotAvailableYet).
longDescription: MongoDB error codes are numeric. `11000` (E11000) duplicate key is the most common write error. Replica set primary failover surfaces as 'not master' errors briefly — retry with retryWrites=true. Transaction abort (251) needs explicit retry logic. Connection failures often trace to SRV record DNS issues with Atlas connection strings.

name: Kubernetes
description: Kubernetes pod statuses: ImagePullBackOff, CrashLoopBackOff, ErrImagePull, OOMKilled, CreateContainerConfigError, RunContainerError. Most issues trace to image pull, resource limits, or config maps/secrets.
longDescription: K8s pod failures cluster around image (ImagePullBackOff, ErrImagePull), runtime (CrashLoopBackOff, RunContainerError), resources (OOMKilled, Evicted), and config (CreateContainerConfigError, InvalidImageName). `kubectl describe pod` and `kubectl logs --previous` are your first two commands. ImagePullBackOff = registry auth, missing tag, or typo.

name: Docker
description: Docker errors: 'no such image', 'driver failed programming external connectivity', 'manifest unknown', exit code 137 (OOM), exit code 139 (segfault), exit code 125 (Docker daemon error).
longDescription: Docker exit codes: 0 success, 1 application error, 125 Docker daemon error, 126 command can't execute, 127 command not found, 137 SIGKILL (OOM), 139 SIGSEGV, 143 SIGTERM. Build errors usually trace to missing build context, RUN command failures, or layer cache invalidation. `docker system prune` is the cure for many storage-related errors.

name: Nginx
description: Nginx errors: 502 Bad Gateway (upstream connect failed), 504 Gateway Timeout (upstream read timeout), 413 Request Entity Too Large, 'too many open files', 'upstream prematurely closed'.
longDescription: Nginx 502/504 distinction: 502 = upstream connect failed (TCP/socket level), 504 = upstream took too long to respond. Tune `proxy_connect_timeout`, `proxy_read_timeout`, `proxy_send_timeout`. 413 needs `client_max_body_size`. 'too many open files' = bump worker_rlimit_nofile and OS ulimit. Always check `error.log` with the request_id from access.log.

name: Node.js
description: Node.js errors: ECONNREFUSED, ETIMEDOUT, EADDRINUSE, ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND, ERR_REQUIRE_ESM, EACCES, UnhandledPromiseRejection, MaxListenersExceededWarning, JavaScript heap out of memory.
longDescription: Node errors split into system (libuv, prefixed E*), V8 (heap, stack), and Node-specific (ERR_*). Most production issues: ESM/CJS interop (ERR_REQUIRE_ESM), port conflicts (EADDRINUSE), unhandled promise rejections (now crash by default), and heap exhaustion on streaming JSON parsing. `--max-old-space-size` is a band-aid; find the leak.

name: Python
description: Python exceptions: ImportError, ModuleNotFoundError, TypeError, ValueError, AttributeError, IndexError, KeyError, FileNotFoundError, ConnectionError, TimeoutError, RecursionError.
longDescription: Python exceptions inherit from BaseException. Most production issues: ModuleNotFoundError (env mismatch, missing requirements), TypeError on async/await misuse, AttributeError on optional fields, ConnectionError on flaky external services. `pip install` in the wrong venv is responsible for an embarrassing share of ImportError reports.

name: Go
description: Go errors aren't exceptions — they're return values. Common patterns: 'connection refused', 'context deadline exceeded', 'panic: runtime error', 'concurrent map writes', 'no such file or directory'.
longDescription: Go errors are values, returned alongside results. Use `errors.Is()` and `errors.As()` for typed checks. `context.DeadlineExceeded` and `context.Canceled` are the two main context errors. Panics on nil map writes, slice out-of-bounds, and concurrent map access are runtime errors that crash unless recovered. Goroutine leaks are silent until they aren't.

name: Rust
description: Rust errors are typed Result<T, E> values, but compile-time issues dominate: borrow checker errors (E0382, E0502), lifetime errors, trait bound errors, type mismatch (E0308). Panics: unwrap on None, index out of bounds.
longDescription: Rust errors split into compile-time (rustc error codes E0001-E0700+) and runtime (panics, Result::Err). Borrow checker errors (E0382 use after move, E0499 mutable borrow conflict) are the steepest learning curve. `?` operator and `thiserror`/`anyhow` are standard for runtime error propagation. `cargo check` is faster than `cargo build` for catching errors.

name: Next.js
description: Next.js errors: 'Hydration failed', 'Module not found', 'getServerSideProps' failures, ISR revalidation errors, 'Image Optimization' errors, 'Failed to compile', 'Error: Connection terminated'.
longDescription: Next.js error sources: hydration mismatches (server vs client HTML), App Router server/client component boundaries, ISR fetch failures, edge runtime constraints (no Node APIs), Image Optimization remote pattern denials. Hydration errors are usually `Date.now()`, `Math.random()`, browser-only APIs, or mismatched HTML tags between server and client renders.

name: Django
description: Django errors: ImproperlyConfigured, OperationalError (database), DoesNotExist, MultipleObjectsReturned, IntegrityError, TemplateDoesNotExist, FieldError, MigrationSchemaMissing.
longDescription: Django errors split into config (ImproperlyConfigured), ORM (DoesNotExist, MultipleObjectsReturned, IntegrityError), template (TemplateDoesNotExist, TemplateSyntaxError), and migration (InconsistentMigrationHistory, NodeNotFoundError). Most production issues are migration drift between dev and prod, or `select_related`/`prefetch_related` missing on hot ORM paths causing N+1.

name: Rails
description: Rails errors: ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid, RoutingError, ActionController::ParameterMissing, NoMethodError on nil, PG::ConnectionBad, deadlock detected.
longDescription: Rails errors mostly come from ActiveRecord (RecordNotFound, RecordInvalid, deadlocks) and routing (RoutingError, AbstractController::ActionNotFound). Strong parameters raise ParameterMissing. Asset compilation errors block deploys. N+1 queries are flagged by `bullet` gem. PG::ConnectionBad usually means too many app instances vs Postgres `max_connections`.

name: Laravel
description: Laravel errors: 419 (CSRF token mismatch), QueryException, ModelNotFoundException, MassAssignmentException, ValidationException, InvalidArgumentException, MethodNotAllowedHttpException.
longDescription: Laravel 419 = expired/invalid CSRF token (session timeout, mismatched cookie domain, stale form). QueryException wraps PDO errors. MassAssignmentException requires updating `$fillable` or `$guarded`. ValidationException returns JSON 422 in API contexts, redirects elsewhere. Queue jobs that fail get retried per `tries` config; check `failed_jobs` table.

name: Express
description: Express errors: 'Cannot set headers after they are sent', 'TypeError: req.body is undefined', 'PayloadTooLargeError', middleware error not caught, missing next() call, async error not propagated.
longDescription: Express's biggest gotcha: async errors aren't caught by the default error middleware. Use express-async-errors or wrap handlers in try/catch with next(err). 'Cannot set headers after they are sent' = double res.send/res.json call, often from missing return after a response. body-parser errors (PayloadTooLargeError) need explicit limits.

name: FastAPI
description: FastAPI errors: RequestValidationError (422), HTTPException (custom), missing dependency injection, async DB session lifecycle errors, CORS preflight failures, JWT validation errors.
longDescription: FastAPI returns 422 with detailed validation errors for malformed inputs (Pydantic). HTTPException is the way to raise typed HTTP errors. Async DB sessions need correct lifecycle (Depends with yield). CORS errors hit on preflight (OPTIONS) — make sure CORSMiddleware is registered before routes. WebSocket close codes (1006, 1011) point at server-side disconnects.
